rulings.Case Title: PHILIP DUNCAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MAY 11, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not submit any admissible evidence creating a triable issue he was exposed to asbestos-containing Cranite sheet gaskets or asbestos-containing packing attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the declaration of Mr. Ay are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CLARENCE DELOUIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MAY 11, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to respirable fibers from original, asbestos-containing bonnet gaskets removed from Crane Co. valves. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and the deposition testimony of Mr. McLean and Mr. Pantaleoni, which were identified and discussed in plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories, preclude defendant from invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Dr. Bruch¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff¿s asbestos-related disease. Defendant¿s objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. McLean and Mr. Pantaleoni are overruled. = (220/HEK)
